steps.title: Substitute the linear equation into the quadratic
steps.content:
We can eliminate $y$ by substituting $y = -6x + 36$ into $x^2 = 6x + y$:
$$x^2 = 6x + (-6x + 36)$$
Simplifying: $$x^2 = 36$$

steps.title: Solve the resulting equation for x
Find y values and compute xy for each solution
steps.content:
Use $y = -6x + 36$ to find the corresponding $y$:
  • When $x = 6$: $y = -6(6) + 36 = -36 + 36 = 0$, so $xy = 0$
  • When $x = -6$: $y = -6(-6) + 36 = 36 + 36 = 72$, so $xy = -432$

Among the given choices, only $xy = 0$ appears, which is choice A — A is correct.
